title: camellia japonica subsp. rusticana
intro: Camellia japonica subsp. rusticana, also known as the Japanese camellia, is a subspecies of Camellia japonica, a flowering plant native to East Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, as well as its ability to lower cholesterol levels and improve cardiovascular health. The health benefits of Camellia japonica subsp. rusticana include reducing the risk of heart disease, improving blood lipid profiles, and possessing antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of this plant, often in the form of tea, are commonly used in traditional medicine to promote overall well-being and support cardiovascular health.
